javadoc_before:
/** * Construct a Newton polynomial with the given a[] and c[]. The order of * centers are important in that if c[] shuffle, then values of a[] would * completely change, not just a permutation of old a[]. *

* The constructor makes copy of the input arrays and assigns them.

* * @param a the coefficients in Newton form formula * @param c the centers * @throws IllegalArgumentException if input arrays are not valid */
javadoc_after:
/** * Construct a Newton polynomial with the given a[] and c[]. The order of * centers are important in that if c[] shuffle, then values of a[] would * completely change, not just a permutation of old a[]. *

* The constructor makes copy of the input arrays and assigns them.

* * @param a Coefficients in Newton form formula. * @param c Centers. * @throws NullArgumentException if any argument is {@code null}. * @throws NoDataException if any array has zero length. * @throws DimensionMismatchException if the size difference between * {@code a} and {@code c} is not equal to 1. */
